name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: A classic flat-bench press that overloads the chest while also training the front delts and triceps.
description: A classic flat-bench press that overloads the chest while also training the front delts and triceps.
howTo: Set up on a flat bench with your eyes under the bar and your feet planted hard on the floor.
Unrack the bar, hold it over your lower chest, and lower it under control until it lightly touches.
Press the bar back up while keeping your upper back tight and your wrists stacked over your elbows.
cues: Pin your shoulder blades into the bench.
Keep your forearms vertical at the bottom.
Drive your feet into the floor as you press.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: An incline press that shifts more of the work toward the upper chest while still hammering the front delts and triceps.
description: An incline press that shifts more of the work toward the upper chest while still hammering the front delts and triceps.
howTo: Set a bench to a low or moderate incline and bring the dumbbells to shoulder level as you lie back.
Press the weights up and slightly inward until your arms are extended without losing your rib position.
Lower until your elbows come just below the bench line, then press again under control.
cues: Keep the bench angle low enough to stay chest-dominant.
Lower with your elbows under your wrists.
Think about driving through the upper chest.

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: A stable chest press that lets you train the pecs hard without having to manage free-weight balance.
description: A stable chest press that lets you train the pecs hard without having to manage free-weight balance.
howTo: Adjust the seat so the handles line up around mid-chest and your shoulders stay relaxed.
Press the handles forward without letting your upper back peel off the pad.
Return slowly until you feel a chest stretch, then press again.
cues: Keep your chest lifted against the pad.
Use a smooth press instead of slamming the stack.
Let the chest stretch at the bottom.

name: Cable Fly
instructions: A chest isolation move that keeps constant cable tension through the pecs instead of turning the set into a press.
description: A chest isolation move that keeps constant cable tension through the pecs instead of turning the set into a press.
howTo: Set the pulleys just above shoulder height and step forward into a staggered stance with a soft bend in the elbows.
Sweep the handles together in a hugging arc until your hands meet in front of your chest.
Open the arms back up slowly until you feel the pecs lengthen, then repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows fixed instead of pressing.
Think about bringing your upper arms together.
Hold the squeeze briefly in front.

name: Push-Up
instructions: A bodyweight press that builds chest, triceps, and trunk control at the same time.
description: A bodyweight press that builds chest, triceps, and trunk control at the same time.
howTo: Set up in a rigid plank with your hands just outside shoulder width and your feet together or slightly apart.
Lower your chest toward the floor while keeping your body in one straight line.
Press back up until your elbows are extended and your shoulder blades spread naturally.
cues: Squeeze your glutes and abs the whole rep.
Bring the chest down between the hands.
Push the floor away hard at the top.

name: Pike Push-Up
instructions: A bodyweight pressing variation that makes the shoulders work harder than a standard push-up.
description: A bodyweight pressing variation that makes the shoulders work harder than a standard push-up.
howTo: Start in a pike position with your hips high and your torso angled downward.
Bend your elbows and lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Press back up by driving through the shoulders until the arms are straight again.
cues: Keep the hips high so the press stays vertical.
Aim the crown of your head between your hands.
Push through the shoulders, not just the elbows.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: A seated overhead press that lets you load the front and side delts with a more controlled torso position.
description: A seated overhead press that lets you load the front and side delts with a more controlled torso position.
howTo: Sit tall on a bench with the dumbbells at shoulder height and your forearms vertical.
Press the weights overhead until your biceps line up near your ears.
Lower under control back to shoulder level without collapsing your torso.
cues: Keep your ribs down as you press.
Finish with the dumbbells stacked over the shoulders.
Lower slowly to keep tension on the delts.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
instructions: A standing overhead press that builds shoulder and triceps strength while demanding full-body bracing.
description: A standing overhead press that builds shoulder and triceps strength while demanding full-body bracing.
howTo: Start with the bar at upper-chest height, elbows slightly in front of the bar, and your glutes squeezed.
Press the bar up in a straight line while moving your head back and then through at lockout.
Lower back to the collarbone area without relaxing your torso.
cues: Brace like you are about to take a punch.
Keep the bar close to your face on the way up.
Finish with the bar over the mid-foot.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
instructions: A delt isolation move that emphasizes the side delts rather than the traps or momentum.
description: A delt isolation move that emphasizes the side delts rather than the traps or momentum.
howTo: Stand tall with a light pair of dumbbells at your sides and a slight bend in the elbows.
Lift the arms out to the sides until the elbows reach about shoulder height.
Lower slowly and keep the tension on the side delts instead of dropping the weights.
cues: Lead with the elbows, not the hands.

name: Face Pull
instructions: A cable upper-back drill that trains rear delts, scapular retraction, and external rotation together.
description: A cable upper-back drill that trains rear delts, scapular retraction, and external rotation together.
howTo: Set a rope at about upper-chest or face height and step back until the cable is taut.
Pull the rope toward your face while flaring the hands apart and driving the elbows back.
Pause in the fully opened position, then return slowly without letting the shoulders dump forward.
cues: Pull the rope apart as you pull it back.
Keep the chest tall and ribs down.
Finish with the hands beside the ears.

name: Cable Triceps Pushdown
instructions: A straightforward cable isolation exercise for the triceps that is easy to load and easy to recover from.
description: A straightforward cable isolation exercise for the triceps that is easy to load and easy to recover from.
howTo: Stand tall with your elbows pinned near your sides and the handle at lower-chest height.
Extend your elbows until your arms are straight and the triceps are fully contracted.
Control the handle back up without letting the elbows drift forward.
cues: Keep the shoulders quiet and the elbows fixed.
Lock out by squeezing the triceps, not by leaning on the stack.
Use a full range on every rep.

name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
instructions: An overhead triceps move that biases the long head by putting the upper arm in a flexed position.
description: An overhead triceps move that biases the long head by putting the upper arm in a flexed position.
howTo: Face away from a low cable or lean slightly forward from a high cable with the rope behind your head.
Keep the upper arms mostly fixed and extend the elbows until your hands move forward and up.
Lower with control into a deep triceps stretch before the next rep.
cues: Keep the elbows pointed generally forward.
Let the triceps stretch at the bottom.
Think about straightening the arms without moving the shoulders.

name: Alternating Dumbbell Curl
instructions: A simple unilateral curl that lets you focus on supination and elbow flexion one arm at a time.
description: A simple unilateral curl that lets you focus on supination and elbow flexion one arm at a time.
howTo: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and your palms facing inward at your sides.
Curl one weight up while rotating the palm to face your shoulder near the top.
Lower it fully before repeating on the other arm.
cues: Keep the elbow close to the body.
Turn the palm up hard near the top.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: A neutral-grip curl that brings the brachialis and brachioradialis into the lift more than a classic supinated curl.
description: A neutral-grip curl that brings the brachialis and brachioradialis into the lift more than a classic supinated curl.
howTo: Stand with the dumbbells at your sides and your palms facing each other.
Curl the weights up without rotating the wrists and keep the elbows tucked in.
Lower slowly all the way back to the start.
cues: Keep the thumbs pointed up the whole rep.
Squeeze the upper arm at the top.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: A vertical pulling staple that builds the lats, upper back, and elbow flexors with your own bodyweight.
description: A vertical pulling staple that builds the lats, upper back, and elbow flexors with your own bodyweight.
howTo: Hang from the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip and your body under control.
Pull your chest toward the bar by driving the elbows down and back.
Lower all the way to a dead hang without losing control.
cues: Start by pulling the shoulders down away from the ears.
Drive the elbows toward your ribs.
Keep the core tight to limit swinging.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: A more accessible vertical pull that lets you train the lats hard before strict pull-ups are ready.
description: A more accessible vertical pull that lets you train the lats hard before strict pull-ups are ready.
howTo: Set the thigh pad snugly and take a grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Pull the bar toward your upper chest while keeping the torso only slightly leaned back.
Let the bar rise under control until the shoulders fully open again.
cues: Lead with the elbows, not the hands.
Keep the chest high at the bottom of the pull.
Reach long at the top without shrugging hard.

name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
instructions: A heavy horizontal pull that trains the lats and upper back while your trunk stays braced in a hinge.
description: A heavy horizontal pull that trains the lats and upper back while your trunk stays braced in a hinge.
howTo: Hinge over with a flat back, soft knees, and the bar hanging around knee level.
Row the bar toward the lower ribs or upper waist without standing up.
Lower back to the hang while keeping the torso angle steady.
cues: Brace your abs before every rep.
Pull the elbows behind you, not up toward the shoulders.
Keep the bar close to the body.

name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: A unilateral row that makes it easy to drive hard through the lats without your lower back limiting the set.
description: A unilateral row that makes it easy to drive hard through the lats without your lower back limiting the set.
howTo: Support yourself on a bench or sturdy surface with the free hand and opposite knee.
Start with the dumbbell hanging straight down and row it toward the hip.
Lower until the arm is long again and the shoulder blade can protract naturally.
cues: Pull the elbow toward the back pocket.
Keep the torso quiet and square.
Reach long at the bottom for a full stretch.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: A controlled machine-based row that is great for building the mid-back and lats through a repeatable path.
description: A controlled machine-based row that is great for building the mid-back and lats through a repeatable path.
howTo: Sit tall with your feet braced, knees soft, and the handle in a strong neutral grip.
Row the handle toward the lower ribs while keeping the chest open.
Extend the arms back out until the shoulder blades can glide forward again.
cues: Lead with the elbows and keep them close to the body.
Stay tall instead of collapsing at the finish.
Let the shoulder blades move naturally.

name: Inverted Row
instructions: A bodyweight row that teaches scapular control and horizontal pulling without loading the lower back.
description: A bodyweight row that teaches scapular control and horizontal pulling without loading the lower back.
howTo: Set a bar around waist height and hang underneath it with your body straight.
Pull your chest to the bar by driving the elbows back and squeezing the upper back.
Lower until the arms are straight again while keeping the body rigid.
cues: Keep your ribs and hips in one line.
Touch the chest to the bar if your setup allows it.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: A posterior-chain hinge that loads the hamstrings hard while teaching you to keep tension through the hips and trunk.
description: A posterior-chain hinge that loads the hamstrings hard while teaching you to keep tension through the hips and trunk.
howTo: Stand tall with the bar in your hands and a soft bend in the knees.
Push your hips back while keeping the bar close to your legs and your spine neutral.
Lower until the hamstrings are stretched, then drive the hips forward to stand tall again.
cues: Keep the bar glued to the thighs and shins.
Think hips back, not chest down.
Stop when the hamstrings run out of room.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: A glute-dominant hinge that lets you create high hip-extension output without heavy spinal loading.
description: A glute-dominant hinge that lets you create high hip-extension output without heavy spinal loading.
howTo: Set your upper back on a bench with the bar settled over the hips and your feet planted.
Drive through the heels and extend the hips until the torso and thighs line up.
Lower the hips under control without losing your rib and pelvis position.
cues: Tuck the ribs down and keep the chin slightly tucked.
Push the floor away through the heels.
Squeeze the glutes hard at the top.

name: Back Squat
instructions: A foundational squat pattern that trains the quads and glutes while demanding strong trunk bracing.
description: A foundational squat pattern that trains the quads and glutes while demanding strong trunk bracing.
howTo: Set the bar on your upper back, brace your torso, and take a stable stance.
Sit down and slightly back while letting the knees track over the toes.
Drive up through the mid-foot until you stand tall again.
cues: Create pressure around your whole trunk before you descend.
Keep the bar stacked over the mid-foot.
Push the floor away out of the bottom.

name: Leg Press
instructions: A machine squat pattern that is easy to learn and useful for driving quad volume with lower balance demands.
description: A machine squat pattern that is easy to learn and useful for driving quad volume with lower balance demands.
howTo: Sit into the sled with your back flat against the pad and your feet around shoulder width on the platform.
Lower the sled until your knees are deeply bent without letting your pelvis roll off the pad.
Press the platform away until the knees are straight but not violently locked.
cues: Control the bottom instead of crashing into it.
Use the quads to drive the sled away.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: A beginner-friendly squat that uses a front-loaded weight to help you stay upright and hit depth cleanly.
description: A beginner-friendly squat that uses a front-loaded weight to help you stay upright and hit depth cleanly.
howTo: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell high against your chest with your elbows angled down.
Sit between your hips while keeping the chest tall and the feet rooted.
Stand back up by driving through the whole foot.
cues: Keep the weight glued to your chest.
Let the knees move forward naturally.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: A hard unilateral leg movement that loads the quads and glutes while exposing side-to-side imbalances.
description: A hard unilateral leg movement that loads the quads and glutes while exposing side-to-side imbalances.
howTo: Place the rear foot on a bench and hop the front foot far enough forward to get a strong stance.
Lower straight down over the front leg until the front thigh is near parallel or deeper if you can control it.
Drive through the front foot to return to the start without bouncing off the back leg.
cues: Own the front leg and keep most of the load there.
Let the front knee travel naturally over the toes.
Stay tall and balanced over the lead foot.

name: Bodyweight Squat
instructions: A simple squat pattern for learning depth, rhythm, and control before you load the movement heavier.
description: A simple squat pattern for learning depth, rhythm, and control before you load the movement heavier.
howTo: Stand with your feet around shoulder width and your arms in front for balance.
Sit down between the hips while keeping the whole foot on the floor.
Stand back up smoothly without collapsing the chest.
cues: Keep the weight through the whole foot.
Reach the hips and knees together.
Stand tall at the top of each rep.

name: Leg Extension
instructions: A pure knee-extension exercise that lets you focus directly on quad tension and lockout strength.
description: A pure knee-extension exercise that lets you focus directly on quad tension and lockout strength.
howTo: Set the machine so your knees line up with the pivot point and the pad sits above the ankles.
Extend the knees until the legs are nearly straight and the quads are fully contracted.
Lower slowly back into the stretch instead of dropping the stack.
cues: Keep your hips pinned into the seat.
Squeeze the quads at the top for a beat.

name: Seated Leg Curl
instructions: A hamstring isolation exercise that trains knee flexion in a lengthened seated position.
description: A hamstring isolation exercise that trains knee flexion in a lengthened seated position.
howTo: Adjust the machine so the back pad supports you and the roller sits just above your heels.
Curl the pad down by pulling your heels under the seat while keeping your hips anchored.
Return slowly until the hamstrings lengthen again.
cues: Press the hips into the pad.
Move through the knees instead of kicking with the calves.
Control the stretch on the way back up.

name: Standing barbell calf raise
instructions: A standing calf variation that emphasizes the gastrocnemius by training plantar flexion with straight knees.
description: A standing calf variation that emphasizes the gastrocnemius by training plantar flexion with straight knees.
howTo: Stand with the bar on your back and the balls of your feet on a step or plate edge if available.
Drop the heels into a stretch, then raise up as high as you can onto the toes.
Pause at the top before lowering back down under control.
cues: Get a full stretch at the bottom.
Reach tall through the ankles at the top.

name: Cable Seated Crunch
instructions: A loaded ab movement that trains spinal flexion through the abs instead of just yanking with the hips.
description: A loaded ab movement that trains spinal flexion through the abs instead of just yanking with the hips.
howTo: Set the rope high, lock it near your temples, and sit or kneel far enough away to keep cable tension.
Curl your ribs toward your pelvis and crunch down through the midsection.
Return slowly until the abs lengthen again without letting the low back take over.
cues: Think ribs to hips, not elbows to knees.

name: Hanging Knee Raise
instructions: A hanging ab exercise that trains the lower abs and anterior core while forcing you to control swing.
description: A hanging ab exercise that trains the lower abs and anterior core while forcing you to control swing.
howTo: Hang from a bar and establish a steady body position before starting the rep.
Tuck the knees up toward the chest by rolling the pelvis slightly under.
Lower the legs without swinging into the next rep.
cues: Lift the knees with the abs, not just the thighs.
Pause briefly at the top if you can.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: An anti-extension core exercise that punishes you if you lose trunk position or chase range you cannot own.
description: An anti-extension core exercise that punishes you if you lose trunk position or chase range you cannot own.
howTo: Start kneeling with your hands on the wheel and your ribs tucked down over your pelvis.
Roll forward slowly while keeping the trunk braced and the hips from sagging.
Stop before you lose position, then pull back in by driving through the abs and lats.
cues: Keep the ribs tucked the whole time.
Reach long without letting the low back collapse.
Think about pulling the wheel back with your abs.

name: Plank
instructions: A basic anti-extension hold that teaches full-body bracing and clean trunk alignment.
description: A basic anti-extension hold that teaches full-body bracing and clean trunk alignment.
howTo: Set your forearms on the floor with elbows under shoulders and legs extended behind you.
Lift into a straight line from head to heel while squeezing the glutes and abs.
Hold the position without letting the hips sag or pike.
cues: Brace like you are about to be punched.
Keep the glutes tight the whole set.
Push the floor away through the forearms.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: A ballistic hinge that develops hip snap, posterior-chain power, and conditioning when done with a true hinge pattern.
description: A ballistic hinge that develops hip snap, posterior-chain power, and conditioning when done with a true hinge pattern.
howTo: Hike the kettlebell back between the legs while keeping a long spine and loaded hips.
Snap the hips forward to float the bell to chest height without lifting it with the arms.
Let the bell fall back into the next hinge and repeat rhythmically.
cues: Treat it like a hinge, not a squat.
Snap the hips and let the bell float.
Keep the arms long and the lats engaged.

name: Farmer's Carry
instructions: A loaded carry that builds grip, trunk stiffness, and posture while forcing you to walk under control.
description: A loaded carry that builds grip, trunk stiffness, and posture while forcing you to walk under control.
howTo: Pick up a pair of heavy implements and stand tall with the shoulders pulled down and back.
Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the ribcage stacked over the pelvis.
Turn carefully if needed and set the implements down with the same control you used to pick them up.
cues: Stay tall and resist leaning side to side.
Crush the handles the whole carry.

name: 3/4 Sit-Up
instructions: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
description: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
howTo: Lie down on the floor and secure your feet. Your legs should be bent at the knees.
Place your hands behind or to the side of your head. You will begin with your back on the ground. This will be your starting position.
Flex your hips and spine to raise your torso toward your knees.
At the top of the contraction your torso should be perpendicular to the ground. Reverse the motion, going only ¾ of the way down.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Move from the trunk, not random momentum.
Exhale into the hard part of the motion.

name: Ab Crunch Machine
instructions: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
description: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
howTo: Select a light resistance and sit down on the ab machine placing your feet under the pads provided and grabbing the top handles. Your arms should be bent at a 90 degree angle as you rest the triceps on the pads provided. This will be your starting position.

At the same time, begin to lift the legs up as you crunch your upper torso. Breathe out as you perform this movement. Tip: Be sure to use a slow and controlled motion. Concentrate on using your abs to move the weight while relaxing your legs and feet.
After a second pause, slowly return to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
cues: Move from the trunk, not random momentum.
Exhale into the hard part of the motion.

name: Advanced Kettlebell Windmill
instructions: A trunk-focused movement that trains abs while teaching you to control position and tension.
description: A trunk-focused movement that trains abs while teaching you to control position and tension.
howTo: Clean and press a kettlebell overhead with one arm.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Keep the non-working arm behind your back and turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the kettlebell.
Lower yourself as far as possible.
Pause for a second and reverse the motion back to the starting position.
cues: Keep the ribs down as you press.
Press in a straight, efficient path.
Finish with the load stacked overhead.

name: Alternate Hammer Curl
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
howTo: Stand up with your torso upright and a dumbbell in each hand being held at arms length. The elbows should be close to the torso.
The palms of the hands should be facing your torso. This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm stationary, curl the right weight forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. Continue the movement until your biceps is fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze the biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Slowly begin to bring the dumbbells back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat the movement with the left hand. This equals one repetition.
Continue alternating in this manner for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Alternate Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
howTo: Sit down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand being held at arms length. Tip: Keep the elbows close to the torso.This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm stationary, curl the right weight forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. As you do so, rotate the hand so that the palm is facing up. Continue the movement until your biceps is fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze the biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Slowly begin to bring the dumbbell back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat the movement with the left hand. This equals one repetition.
Continue alternating in this manner for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Alternate Leg Diagonal Bound
instructions: An explosive drill that trains quads while teaching you to create and absorb force cleanly.
description: An explosive drill that trains quads while teaching you to create and absorb force cleanly.
howTo: Assume a comfortable stance with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Begin by pushing off with the front leg, driving the opposite knee forward and as high as possible before landing. Attempt to cover as much distance to each side with each bound.
It may help to use a line on the ground to guage distance from side to side.
Repeat the sequence with the other leg.
cues: Explode through the floor on each rep.
Reset your balance before the next effort.

name: Alternating Cable Shoulder Press
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on shoulders and keeps triceps involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on shoulders and keeps triceps involved through the full range.
howTo: Move the cables to the bottom of the tower and select an appropriate weight.
Grasp the cables and hold them at shoulder height, palms facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your head and chest up, extend through the elbow to press one side directly over head.
After pausing at the top, return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
cues: Keep the ribs down as you press.
Press in a straight, efficient path.
Finish with the load stacked overhead.

name: Alternating Deltoid Raise
instructions: This movement loads shoulders and uses supporting muscles to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads shoulders and uses supporting muscles to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: In a standing position, hold a pair of dumbbells at your side.
Keeping your elbows slightly bent, raise the weights directly in front of you to shoulder height, avoiding any swinging or cheating.
Return the weights to your side.
On the next repetition, raise the weights laterally, raising them out to your side to about shoulder height.
Return the weights to the starting position and continue alternating to the front and side.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Alternating Hang Clean
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes hamstrings with help from biceps, calves.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes hamstrings with help from biceps, calves.
howTo: Place two kettlebells between your feet. To get in the starting position, push your butt back and look straight ahead.

Clean one kettlebell to your shoulder and hold on to the other kettlebell in a hanging position. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrist as you do so.
Lower the cleaned kettlebell to a hanging position and clean the alternate kettlebell. Repeat.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Press
instructions: This movement loads shoulders and uses triceps to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads shoulders and uses triceps to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Clean two kettlebells to your shoulders. Clean the kettlebells to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebells towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrists as you do so.
Press one directly overhead by extending through the elbow, turning it so the palm faces forward while holding the other kettlebell stationary .
Lower the pressed kettlebell to the starting position and immediately press with your other arm.
cues: Keep the ribs down as you press.
Press in a straight, efficient path.
Finish with the load stacked overhead.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Row
instructions: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Place two kettlebells in front of your feet. Bend your knees slightly and push your butt out as much as possible. As you bend over to get into the starting position grab both kettlebells by the handles.

Pull one kettlebell off of the floor while holding on to the other kettlebell. Retract the shoulder blade of the working side, as you flex the elbow, drawing the kettlebell towards your stomach or rib cage.
Lower the kettlebell in the working arm and repeat with your other arm.
cues: Keep the chest organized and tall.

name: Atlas Stone Trainer
instructions: A strongman-style exercise that challenges spinal erectors while demanding full-body tension and control.
description: A strongman-style exercise that challenges spinal erectors while demanding full-body tension and control.
howTo: This trainer is effective for developing Atlas Stone strength for those who don't have access to stones, and are typically made from bar ends or heavy pipe.
Begin by loading the desired weight onto the bar. Straddle the weight, wrapping your arms around the implement, bending at the hips.

Begin by pulling the weight up past the knees, extending through the hips. As the weight clears the knees, it can be lapped by resting it on your thighs and sitting back, hugging it tightly to your chest.
Finish the movement by extending through your hips and knees to raise the weight as high as possible. The weight can be returned to the lap or to the ground for successive repetitions.
cues: Brace hard before you move the load.
Keep your posture tall under fatigue.
Take controlled steps or pulls instead of rushing.

name: Atlas Stones
instructions: A loaded event-style movement that makes spinal erectors work hard while the rest of the body stabilizes.
description: A loaded event-style movement that makes spinal erectors work hard while the rest of the body stabilizes.
howTo: Begin with the atlas stone between your feet. Bend at the hips to wrap your arms vertically around the Atlas Stone, attempting to get your fingers underneath the stone. Many stones will have a small flat portion on the bottom, which will make the stone easier to hold.
Pulling the stone into your torso, drive through the back half of your feet to pull the stone from the ground.
As the stone passes the knees, lap it by sitting backward, pulling the stone on top of your thighs.

Sit low, getting the stone high onto your chest as you change your grip to reach over the stone. Stand, driving through with your hips. Close distance to the loading platform, and lean back, extending the hips to get the stone as high as possible.
cues: Brace hard before you move the load.
Keep your posture tall under fatigue.
Take controlled steps or pulls instead of rushing.

name: Axle Deadlift
instructions: A strongman-style exercise that challenges spinal erectors while demanding full-body tension and control.
description: A strongman-style exercise that challenges spinal erectors while demanding full-body tension and control.
howTo: Approach the bar so that it is centered over your feet. You feet should be about hip width apart. Bend at the hip to grip the bar at shoulder width, allowing your shoulder blades to protract. Typically, you would use an over/under grip.

With your feet and your grip set, take a big breath and then lower your hips and flex the knees until your shins contact the bar. Look forward with your head, keep your chest up and your back arched, and begin driving through the heels to move the weight upward.
After the bar passes the knees, aggressively pull the bar back, pulling your shoulder blades together as you drive your hips forward into the bar.
Lower the bar by bending at the hips and guiding it to the floor.
cues: Brace hard before you move the load.
Keep your posture tall under fatigue.
Take controlled steps or pulls instead of rushing.

name: Back Flyes - With Bands
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes shoulders with help from upper back, triceps.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes shoulders with help from upper back, triceps.
howTo: Run a band around a stationary post like that of a squat rack.
Grab the band by the handles and stand back so that the tension in the band rises.

Extend and lift the arms straight in front of you. Tip: Your arms should be straight and parallel to the floor while perpendicular to your torso. Your feet should be firmly planted on the floor spread at shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale, move your arms to the sides and back. Keep your arms extended and parallel to the floor. Continue the movement until the arms are extended to your sides.
After a pause, go back to the original position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Backward Drag
instructions: A strongman-style exercise that challenges quads while demanding full-body tension and control.
description: A strongman-style exercise that challenges quads while demanding full-body tension and control.
howTo: Load a sled with the desired weight, attaching a rope or straps to the sled that you can hold onto.
Begin the exercise by moving backwards for a given distance. Leaning back, extend through the legs for short steps to move as quickly as possible.
cues: Brace hard before you move the load.
Keep your posture tall under fatigue.
Take controlled steps or pulls instead of rushing.

name: Backward Medicine Ball Throw
instructions: An explosive drill that trains shoulders while teaching you to create and absorb force cleanly.
description: An explosive drill that trains shoulders while teaching you to create and absorb force cleanly.
howTo: This exercise is best done with a partner. If you lack a partner, the ball can be thrown and retrieved or thrown against a wall.
Begin standing a few meters in front of your partner, both facing the same direction. Begin holding the ball between your legs.
Squat down and then forcefully reverse direction, coming to full extension and you toss the ball over your head to your partner.
Your partner can then roll the ball back to you. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
cues: Explode through the floor on each rep.
Reset your balance before the next effort.

name: Balance Board
instructions: This movement loads calves and uses hamstrings, quads to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads calves and uses hamstrings, quads to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Place a balance board in front of you.
Stand up on it and try to balance yourself.
Hold the balance for as long as desired.
cues: Stay organized from rep to rep.
Use the target muscles to drive the movement.

name: Band Good Morning (Pull Through)
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes hamstrings with help from glutes, spinal erectors.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes hamstrings with help from glutes, spinal erectors.
howTo: Loop the band around a post. Standing a little ways away, loop the opposite end around the neck. Your hands can help hold the band in position.
Begin by bending at the hips, getting your butt back as far as possible. Keep your back flat and bend forward to about 90 degrees. Your knees should be only slightly bent.
Return to the starting position be driving through with the hips to come back to a standing position.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Band Hip Adductions
instructions: This variation trains adductors first, while supporting muscles help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains adductors first, while supporting muscles help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Anchor a band around a solid post or other object.
Stand with your left side to the post, and put your right foot through the band, getting it around the ankle.
Stand up straight and hold onto the post if needed. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the knee straight, raise your right legs out to the side as far as you can.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired rep count.
Switch sides.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Band Pull Apart
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes shoulders with help from upper back, traps.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes shoulders with help from upper back, traps.
howTo: Begin with your arms extended straight out in front of you, holding the band with both hands.
Initiate the movement by performing a reverse fly motion, moving your hands out laterally to your sides.
Keep your elbows extended as you perform the movement, bringing the band to your chest. Ensure that you keep your shoulders back during the exercise.
Pause as you complete the movement, returning to the starting position under control.
cues: Keep the chest organized and tall.

name: Band Good Morning
instructions: This variation trains hamstrings first, while glutes, spinal erectors help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains hamstrings first, while glutes, spinal erectors help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Using a 41 inch band, stand on one end, spreading your feet a small amount. Bend at the hips to loop the end of the band behind your neck. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your legs straight, extend through the hips to come to a near vertical position.
Ensure that you do not round your back as you go down back to the starting position.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Band Assisted Pull-Up
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on lats and keeps abs, forearms involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on lats and keeps abs, forearms involved through the full range.
howTo: Choke the band around the center of the pullup bar. You can use different bands to provide varying levels of assistance.
Pull the end of the band down, and place one bent knee into the loop, ensuring it won't slip out. Take a medium to wide grip on the bar. This will be your starting position.

Pull yourself upward by contracting the lats as you flex the elbow. The elbow should be driven to your side. Pull to the front, attempting to get your chin over the bar. Avoid swinging or jerking movements.
After a brief pause, return to the starting position.
cues: Drive the elbows down through the rep.
Keep the shoulders away from the ears.
Reach long at the top without losing control.

name: Barbell Ab Rollout - On Knees
instructions: A core exercise built around abs, with spinal erectors, shoulders helping you stay organized through the rep.
description: A core exercise built around abs, with spinal erectors, shoulders helping you stay organized through the rep.
howTo: Hold an Olympic barbell loaded with 5-10lbs on each side and kneel on the floor.
Now place the barbell on the floor in front of you so that you are on all your hands and knees (as in a kneeling push up position). This will be your starting position.

Slowly roll the barbell straight forward, stretching your body into a straight position. Tip: Go down as far as you can without touching the floor with your body. Breathe in during this portion of the movement.
After a second pause at the stretched position, start pulling yourself back to the starting position as you breathe out. Tip: Go slowly and keep your abs tight at all times.
cues: Move from the trunk, not random momentum.
Exhale into the hard part of the motion.

name: Barbell Bench Press - Medium Grip
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
howTo: Lie back on a flat bench. Using a medium width grip (a grip that creates a 90-degree angle in the middle of the movement between the forearms and the upper arms), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked. This will be your starting position.
From the starting position, breathe in and begin coming down slowly until the bar touches your middle chest.

After a brief pause, push the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out. Focus on pushing the bar using your chest muscles. Lock your arms and squeeze your chest in the contracted position at the top of the motion, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Tip: Ideally, lowering the weight should take about twice as long as raising it.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
howTo: Stand up with your torso upright while holding a barbell at a shoulder-width grip. The palm of your hands should be facing forward and the elbows should be close to the torso. This will be your starting position.
While holding the upper arms stationary, curl the weights forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second and squeeze the biceps hard.
Slowly begin to bring the bar back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Barbell Curls Lying Against An Incline
instructions: This variation trains biceps first, while supporting muscles help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains biceps first, while supporting muscles help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Lie against an incline bench, with your arms holding a barbell and hanging down in a horizontal line. This will be your starting position.

While keeping the upper arms stationary, curl the weight up as high as you can while squeezing the biceps. Breathe out as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: Only the forearms should move. Do not swing the arms.
After a second contraction, slowly go back to the starting position as you inhale. Tip: Make sure that you go all of the way down.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions: This variation trains spinal erectors first, while calves, forearms help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains spinal erectors first, while calves, forearms help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell.

While keeping the back as straight as possible, bend your knees, bend forward and grasp the bar using a medium (shoulder width) overhand grip. This will be the starting position of the exercise. Tip: If it is difficult to hold on to the bar with this grip, alternate your grip or use wrist straps.

While holding the bar, start the lift by pushing with your legs while simultaneously getting your torso to the upright position as you breathe out. In the upright position, stick your chest out and contract the back by bringing the shoulder blades back. Think of how the soldiers in the military look when they are in standing in attention.

Go back to the starting position by bending at the knees while simultaneously leaning the torso forward at the waist while keeping the back straight. When the weights on the bar touch the floor you are back at the starting position and ready to perform another repetition.
Perform the amount of repetitions prescribed in the program.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Barbell Full Squat
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
howTo: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first set the bar on a rack just above shoulder level. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder-width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees and sitting back with your hips as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until your hamstrings are on your calves. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel or middle of your foot as you straighten the legs and extend the hips to go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Guillotine Bench Press
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
howTo: Using a medium width grip (a grip that creates a 90-degree angle in the middle of the movement between the forearms and the upper arms), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over your neck with your arms locked. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, bring the bar down slowly until it is about 1 inch from your neck.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles. Lock your arms and squeeze your chest in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Press - Medium Grip
instructions: This movement loads chest and uses shoulders, triceps to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads chest and uses shoulders, triceps to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Lie back on an incline bench. Using a medium-width grip (a grip that creates a 90-degree angle in the middle of the movement between the forearms and the upper arms), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on you upper chest.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles. Lock your arms in the contracted position, squeeze your chest, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Tip: it should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Lunge
instructions: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first set the bar on a rack just below shoulder level. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and step forward with your right leg and squat down through your hips, while keeping the torso upright and maintaining balance. Inhale as you go down. Note: Do not allow your knee to go forward beyond your toes as you come down, as this will put undue stress on the knee joint. li>
Using mainly the heel of your foot, push up and go back to the starting position as you exhale.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and then perform with the left leg.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on calves and keeps the supporting muscles involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on calves and keeps the supporting muscles involved through the full range.
howTo: Place a block about 12 inches in front of a flat bench.
Sit on the bench and place the ball of your feet on the block.
Have someone place a barbell over your upper thighs about 3 inches above your knees and hold it there. This will be your starting position.
Raise up on your toes as high as possible as you squeeze the calves and as you breathe out.
After a second contraction, slowly go back to the starting position. Tip: To get maximum benefit stretch your calves as far as you can.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Shoulder Press
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on shoulders and keeps chest, triceps involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on shoulders and keeps chest, triceps involved through the full range.
howTo: Sit on a bench with back support in a squat rack. Position a barbell at a height that is just above your head. Grab the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing forward).

Once you pick up the barbell with the correct grip width, lift the bar up over your head by locking your arms. Hold at about shoulder level and slightly in front of your head. This is your starting position.
Lower the bar down to the shoulders slowly as you inhale.
Lift the bar back up to the starting position as you exhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Side Bend
instructions: A trunk-focused movement that trains abs while teaching you to control position and tension.
description: A trunk-focused movement that trains abs while teaching you to control position and tension.
howTo: Stand up straight while holding a barbell placed on the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck). Your feet should be shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.

While keeping your back straight and your head up, bend only at the waist to the right as far as possible. Breathe in as you bend to the side. Then hold for a second and come back up to the starting position as you exhale. Tip: Keep the rest of the body stationary.
Now repeat the movement but bending to the left instead. Hold for a second and come back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Move from the trunk, not random momentum.
Exhale into the hard part of the motion.

name: Barbell Squat
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
howTo: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first set the bar on a rack to just below shoulder level. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times and also maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position. (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees and hips as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Squat
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
howTo: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first set the bar on a rack to just below shoulder level. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times and also maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position. (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees and hips as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Squat To A Bench
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, glutes involved through the full range.
howTo: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first place a flat bench or a box behind you. The flat bench is used to teach you to set your hips back and to hit depth.

Then, set the bar on a rack that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position. (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees and sitting your hips back as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until you slightly touch the bench behind you. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot as you straighten the legs and extend the hips to go back to the starting position.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes biceps with help from forearms.
howTo: Stand up with your torso upright while holding a barbell at a shoulder-width grip. The palm of your hands should be facing forward and the elbows should be close to the torso. This will be your starting position.
While holding the upper arms stationary, curl the weights forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second and squeeze the biceps hard.
Slowly begin to bring the bar back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions: This variation trains spinal erectors first, while calves, forearms help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains spinal erectors first, while calves, forearms help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Stand in front of a loaded barbell.

While keeping the back as straight as possible, bend your knees, bend forward and grasp the bar using a medium (shoulder width) overhand grip. This will be the starting position of the exercise. Tip: If it is difficult to hold on to the bar with this grip, alternate your grip or use wrist straps.

While holding the bar, start the lift by pushing with your legs while simultaneously getting your torso to the upright position as you breathe out. In the upright position, stick your chest out and contract the back by bringing the shoulder blades back. Think of how the soldiers in the military look when they are in standing in attention.

Go back to the starting position by bending at the knees while simultaneously leaning the torso forward at the waist while keeping the back straight. When the weights on the bar touch the floor you are back at the starting position and ready to perform another repetition.
Perform the amount of repetitions prescribed in the program.
cues: Keep the load close to the body.
Finish with the glutes instead of the low back.

name: Barbell Glute Bridge
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on glutes and keeps calves, hamstrings involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on glutes and keeps calves, hamstrings involved through the full range.
howTo: Begin seated on the ground with a loaded barbell over your legs. Using a fat bar or having a pad on the bar can greatly reduce the discomfort caused by this exercise. Roll the bar so that it is directly above your hips, and lay down flat on the floor.
Begin the movement by driving through with your heels, extending your hips vertically through the bar. Your weight should be supported by your upper back and the heels of your feet.
Extend as far as possible, then reverse the motion to return to the starting position.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Hack Squat
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, forearms involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, forearms involved through the full range.
howTo: Stand up straight while holding a barbell behind you at arms length and your feet at shoulder width. Tip: A shoulder width grip is best with the palms of your hands facing back. You can use wrist wraps for this exercise for a better grip. This will be your starting position.
While keeping your head and eyes up and back straight, squat until your upper thighs are parallel to the floor. Breathe in as you slowly go down.
Pressing mainly with the heel of the foot and squeezing the thighs, go back up as you breathe out.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes traps with help from the supporting musculature.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes traps with help from the supporting musculature.
howTo: Stand up straight with your feet at shoulder width as you hold a barbell with both hands in front of you using a pronated grip (palms facing the thighs). Tip: Your hands should be a little wider than shoulder width apart. You can use wrist wraps for this exercise for a better grip. This will be your starting position.
Raise your shoulders up as far as you can go as you breathe out and hold the contraction for a second. Tip: Refrain from trying to lift the barbell by using your biceps.
Slowly return to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Barbell Side Split Squat
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, hamstrings involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on quads and keeps calves, hamstrings involved through the full range.
howTo: Stand up straight while holding a barbell placed on the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck). Your feet should be placed wide apart with the foot of the lead leg angled out to the side. This will be your starting position.
Lower your body towards the side of your angled foot by bending the knee and hip of your lead leg and while keeping the opposite leg only slightly bent. Breathe in as you lower your body.
Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the lead leg. Breathe out as you perform this movement.
After performing the recommended amount of reps, repeat the movement with the opposite leg.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Barbell Walking Lunge
instructions: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Begin standing with your feet shoulder width apart and a barbell across your upper back.

Step forward with one leg, flexing the knees to drop your hips. Descend until your rear knee nearly touches the ground. Your posture should remain upright, and your front knee should stay above the front foot.
Drive through the heel of your lead foot and extend both knees to raise yourself back up.
Step forward with your rear foot, repeating the lunge on the opposite leg.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Bench Dips
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on triceps and keeps chest, shoulders involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on triceps and keeps chest, shoulders involved through the full range.
howTo: For this exercise you will need to place a bench behind your back. With the bench perpendicular to your body, and while looking away from it, hold on to the bench on its edge with the hands fully extended, separated at shoulder width. The legs will be extended forward, bent at the waist and perpendicular to your torso. This will be your starting position.

Slowly lower your body as you inhale by bending at the elbows until you lower yourself far enough to where there is an angle slightly smaller than 90 degrees between the upper arm and the forearm. Tip: Keep the elbows as close as possible throughout the movement. Forearms should always be pointing down.
Using your triceps to bring your torso up again, lift yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Bench Press - Powerlifting
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on triceps and keeps chest, forearms involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on triceps and keeps chest, forearms involved through the full range.
howTo: Begin by lying on the bench, getting your head beyond the bar if possible. Tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement.
However wide your grip, it should cover the ring on the bar. Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart.
Lower the bar to your lower chest or upper stomach. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times.
Pause when the barbell touches your torso, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Bench Press - With Bands
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps shoulders, triceps involved through the full range.
howTo: Using a flat bench secure a band under the leg of the bench that is nearest to your head.
Once the band is secure, grab it by both handles and lie down on the bench.
Extend your arms so that you are holding the band handles in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
Bring down the handles slowly until your elbow forms a 90 degree angle. Keep full control at all times.

As you breathe out, bring the handles up using your pectoral muscles. Lock your arms in the contracted position, squeeze your chest, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly. Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Bench Press with Chains
instructions: This movement loads triceps and uses chest, lats to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads triceps and uses chest, lats to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Adjust the leader chain, shortening it to the desired length.Place the chains on the sleeves of the bar.

Lying on the bench, get your head beyond the bar if possible. Tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement. However wide your grip, it should cover the ring on the bar.

Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart. Lower the bar to your lower chest or upper stomach. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times.
Pause when the barbell touches your torso, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Bent Over Barbell Row
instructions: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Holding a barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing down), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward, by bending at the waist, while keeping the back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep the head up. The barbell should hang directly in front of you as your arms hang perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

Now, while keeping the torso stationary, breathe out and lift the barbell to you. Keep the elbows close to the body and only use the forearms to hold the weight. At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a brief pause.
Then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the chest organized and tall.

name: Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In
instructions: A pulling exercise that emphasizes upper back with help from biceps, lats.
description: A pulling exercise that emphasizes upper back with help from biceps, lats.
howTo: With a dumbbell in each hand (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward, by bending at the waist, while keeping the back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep the head up. The weights should hang directly in front of you as your arms hang perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the dumbbells to your side as you breathe out, squeezing your shoulder blades together. On the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the weight again to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the chest organized and tall.

name: Bent Over Barbell Row
instructions: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
description: This variation trains upper back first, while biceps, lats help you finish each rep cleanly.
howTo: Holding a barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing down), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward, by bending at the waist, while keeping the back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep the head up. The barbell should hang directly in front of you as your arms hang perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

Now, while keeping the torso stationary, breathe out and lift the barbell to you. Keep the elbows close to the body and only use the forearms to hold the weight. At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a brief pause.
Then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
cues: Keep the chest organized and tall.

name: Bicycling
instructions: A isolation movement using other that emphasizes quads while calves, glutes assist.
description: A isolation movement using other that emphasizes quads while calves, glutes assist.
howTo: To begin, seat yourself on the bike and adjust the seat to your height.
cues: Keep the target joint doing the work.
Stay smooth instead of chasing momentum.
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscle.

name: Bodyweight Flyes
instructions: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps abs, shoulders involved through the full range.
description: A pushing exercise that focuses on chest and keeps abs, shoulders involved through the full range.
howTo: Position two equally loaded EZ bars on the ground next to each other. Ensure they are able to roll.
Assume a push-up position over the bars, supporting your weight on your toes and hands with your arms extended and body straight.
Place your hands on the bars. This will be your starting position.
Using a slow and controlled motion, move your hands away from the midline of your body, rolling the bars apart. Inhale during this portion of the motion.
After moving the bars as far apart as you can, return to the starting position by pulling them back together. Exhale as you perform this movement.
cues: Keep the upper back set and stable.
Stack the wrists over the elbows.

name: Bodyweight Walking Lunge
instructions: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Begin standing with your feet shoulder width apart and your hands on your hips.

Step forward with one leg, flexing the knees to drop your hips. Descend until your rear knee nearly touches the ground. Your posture should remain upright, and your front knee should stay above the front foot.
Drive through the heel of your lead foot and extend both knees to raise yourself back up.
Step forward with your rear foot, repeating the lunge on the opposite leg.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Bodyweight Walking Lunge
instructions: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
description: This movement loads quads and uses calves, glutes to keep the rep strong and stable.
howTo: Begin standing with your feet shoulder width apart and your hands on your hips.

Step forward with one leg, flexing the knees to drop your hips. Descend until your rear knee nearly touches the ground. Your posture should remain upright, and your front knee should stay above the front foot.
Drive through the heel of your lead foot and extend both knees to raise yourself back up.
Step forward with your rear foot, repeating the lunge on the opposite leg.
cues: Drive through the floor to stand up.

name: Bosu Ball Cable Crunch With Side Bends
instructions: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
description: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
howTo: Connect a standard handle to each arm of a cable machine, and position them in the most downward position.
Grab a Bosu Ball and position it in front and center of the cable machine.
Lie down on the Bosu Ball with the small of your back arched around the ball. Your rear end should be close to the floor without touching it.
With both hands, reach back and grab the handle of each cable.
With your feet positioned in a wide stance, extend your arms straight out in front of you and in between your knees. Your hands should be at knee level.
Keep your arms straight and in-line with the upward angle of the cable. Elevate your torso in a crunching motion without dropping or bending your arms.
cues: Move from the trunk, not random momentum.
Exhale into the hard part of the motion.

name: Bottoms Up
instructions: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
description: A core exercise built around abs, with supporting muscles helping you stay organized through the rep.
howTo: Begin by lying on your back on the ground. Your legs should be straight and your arms at your side. This will be your starting position.
